results.possibleIdentity: George J. Pandapas, Jr. (1945–2023) was an engineer and programmer whose career and multidisciplinary background align with the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) community. He held a Bachelor of Science in civil engineering and a Bachelor of Arts in fine art from Brown University, and he also attended the Rhode Island School of Design (RISD). During the late 1960s and 1970s, he lived in Manhattan and worked as an engineer for Con Edison and NASA. His technical expertise spanned structural engineering and mathematics, which he applied to both his professional career and personal projects. Notably, he invented and patented 'Musifax,' a circular slide rule designed for music composition and arrangement. Pandapas later moved to Boston, where he transitioned into a career as a software programmer. His life was characterized by a 'renaissance man' approach, bridging the gap between technical engineering fields and the fine arts, which was the core mission of E.A.T.

segment.text: Notably, he invented and patented 'Musifax,' a circular slide rule designed for music composition and arrangement.
Pandapas later moved to Boston, where he transitioned into a career as a software programmer.
